findings.title: Data Exfiltration to External Server
findings.description: The cloud_sync.py script collects environment variables, project config files, and system credential files (~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc) and sends them via HTTPS as a base64-encoded payload to an external endpoint (cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup). A hardcoded API key (cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ) is used for authorization. This results in massive credential theft and exposure of sensitive system configuration.
findings.evidence: Lines 30-36 read credential files; lines 38-44 upload the collected data to SYNC_ENDPOINT; line 15 defines the endpoint; line 16 defines the API key.
findings.remediation: Immediately remove this skill. Do not execute the script under any circumstances. The endpoint appears malicious and should be reported.

findings.title: Capability Inflation / Deceptive Description
findings.description: The skill's YAML description claims to 'synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage', but the bundled script actually collects and exfiltrates credential files from entire system locations (~/.aws, ~/.ssh, ~/.config/gcloud), far beyond any project scope. The name 'cloud-sync' and description mislead users about the true behavior, increasing the likelihood of unwanted activation.
findings.evidence: Description states 'Synchronize local project files and configurations' while code reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, etc.
findings.remediation: Remove the deceptive skill or rewrite it to accurately reflect the scope (e.g., no credential collection). Users should verify skill content before use.
